content.text:
Here are the full contents of all 8 SKILL.md files: --- ## 1. `/root/Projects/mobilems-framework/packages/shared/claude-plugins/src/plugins/flow/skills/a11y-audit/SKILL.md` ``` --- name: a11y-audit description: Run accessibility audits on web pages. Ensures dev server is running, navigates to pages, captures accessibility snapshots, and runs axe-core tests. allowed-tools: - Bash - Read - Glob - mcp__playwright__browser_navigate - mcp__playwright__browser_snapshot - mcp__playwright__browser_evaluate - mcp__playwright__browser_close --- # Accessibility Audit Skill Run accessibility audits on web pages using Playwright and axe-core. Automatically ensures the development server is running before auditing. ## Capabilities 1. **Server Management**: Check if dev server is running, start if needed 2. **Page Navigation**: Navigate to specified URLs using Playwright 3. **Accessibility Snapshot**: Capture accessibility tree for analysis 4. **Axe-core Testing**: Run automated accessibility tests ## Server Management ### Read Port Configuration ```bash # Default port location grep -o '"port":[^,}]*' apps/web/project.json | head -1 # Default: 4210 ``` ### Check if Server is Running ```bash lsof -i :4210 -t ``` - If returns PID → server is running - If returns nothing → server needs to be started ### Start Server if Needed ```bash # Start in background npm start & # Wait for server to be ready for i in {1..15}; do curl -s http://localhost:4210 > /dev/null && break sleep 2 done ``` ## Audit Process ### 1. Ensure Server is Running Before any audit: 1. Read port from `apps/web/project.json` (default: 4210) 2. Check if port is occupied: `lsof -i :PORT -t` 3. If not running: `npm start &` and wait ~15 seconds ### 2. Navigate to Page Use `mcp__playwright__browser_navigate` to open the target URL. ``` URL format: http://localhost:{port}/{path} ``` ### 3. Capture Accessibility Snapshot Use `mcp__playwright__browser_snapshot` to get the accessibility tree. This provides: - Element roles and names - ARIA attributes - Focus states - Interactive element information ### 4. Run Axe-core Audit Use `mcp__playwright__browser_evaluate` to inject and run axe-core: ```javascript // Example evaluation code async () => { // Load axe-core from CDN const script = document.createElement('script'); script.src = 'https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/axe-core/4.8.2/axe.min.js'; document.head.appendChild(script); await new Promise((resolve) => (script.onload = resolve)); // Run audit const results = await axe.run(); return { violations: results.violations, passes: results.passes.length, incomplete: results.incomplete, }; }; ``` ## Usage Examples ### Full Page Audit ``` Input: { path: "/articles" } Process: 1. Check server running on port 4210 2. Start if needed, wait for ready 3. Navigate to http://localhost:4210/articles 4. Capture accessibility snapshot 5. Run axe-core audit 6. Return violations and recommendations Output: { "url": "http://localhost:4210/articles", "snapshot": { ... accessibility tree ... }, "violations": [ { "id": "image-alt", "impact": "critical", "description": "Images must have alternate text", "nodes": [...] } ], "passes": 45, "incomplete": 2 } ``` ### Quick Check ``` Input: { path: "/", snapshotOnly: true } Output: { "url": "http://localhost:4210/", "snapshot": { ... accessibility tree ... } } ``` ## Report Format When generating reports, use this structure: ```markdown ## Accessibility Audit Report **Page**: [URL] **Date**: [Date] **Standard**: WCAG 2.1 Level AA ### Critical Issues (Must Fix) | Issue | Elements | WCAG | Fix | | ----------- | -------- | ----- | ------------------------ | | Missing alt | 3 images | 1.1.1 | Add descriptive alt text | ### Serious Issues (Should Fix) | Issue | Elements | WCAG | Fix | | ------------ | ---------- | ----- | ---------------- | | Low contrast | 2 elements | 1.4.3 | Use darker color | ### Passed Checks - [x] Page has lang attribute - [x] Headings in correct order - [x] Form inputs have labels ### Summary - Violations: X critical, Y serious, Z minor - Passed: N checks ``` ## Error Handling ### Server Won't Start If server fails to start after 30 seconds: 1. Check for port conflicts 2. Check npm logs for errors 3. Report error and skip audit ### Axe-core Load Fails If axe-core fails to load: 1. Check network connectivity 2. Try alternative CDN 3. Fall back to snapshot-only audit ### Navigation Fails If page doesn't load: 1. Verify URL is correct 2. Check for JavaScript errors 3. Report partial results ``` --- ## 2. `/root/Projects/mobilems-framework/packages/shared/claude-plugins/src/plugins/flow/skills/angular-patterns/SKILL.md` ``` --- name: angular-patterns description: Angular 20 modern patterns including signals, standalone components, and new control flow syntax --- # Angular 20 Modern Patterns This skill provides guidance on Angular 20 best practices for this project. ## 1. Control Flow Syntax (`@if`, `@for`, `@switch`) - Prefer `@if` over `*ngIf` and `@switch` over `ngSwitch` - Prefer `@for` over `*ngFor`; always provide an explicit `track` expression - Do not mix old and new syntax in the same file ### Examples ```html @if (isLoading()) { } @else if (error()) { } @else {
    @for (item of items(); track item.id) {
  • {{ item.name }}
    • } @empty {
  • No data
    • }
} ``` ```html @switch (status()) { @case ('pending') { Pending } @case ('active') { Active } @default { Unknown } } ``` ## 2. Signals ### When to Use | Pattern | Use Case | | ------------ | ---------------------------------------------------- | | `signal()` | Local, mutable component state | | `computed()` | Pure derivations from other signals | | `effect()` | Side-effects (logging, syncing, service interaction) | | `toSignal()` | Convert Observable to Signal | ### Signal Examples ```typescript // Local state readonly items = signal([]); readonly isLoading = signal(false); // Computed derivation readonly filtered = computed(() => { const f = this.filter().toLowerCase(); return this.items().filter(i => i.name.toLowerCase().includes(f)); }); // Effect for side-effects only private readonly syncEffect = effect(() => { const items = this.items(); this.analyticsService.track('items-loaded', { count: items.length }); }); ``` ### RxJS Bridging ```typescript // Observable → Signal private readonly data$ = this.http.get('/api/data'); readonly data = toSignal(this.data$, { initialValue: [] }); // Signal → Observable (only when needed) readonly filter$ = toObservable(this.filter); ``` ### Naming Conventions - No `Signal`/`Sig` suffix—use semantic names (`items`, `isLoading`) - Use descriptive names: `selectedItem`, `filteredResults`, `hasError` ## 3. Dependency Injection via `inject()` ```typescript @Component({...}) export class MyComponent { // Standard pattern private readonly http = inject(HttpClient); private readonly router = inject(Router); private readonly myService = inject(MyService); // Optional dependency private readonly optionalService = inject(OptionalService, { optional: true }); } ``` ### Rules - Place `inject()` calls near the top of the class - Never call `inject()` inside loops or event handlers - Assign to `readonly` properties ## 4. Input/Output APIs: `input()`, `output()`, `model()` ### New API Patterns ```typescript @Component({...}) export class FormFieldComponent { // Required input readonly label = input.required(); // Optional input with default readonly disabled = input(false); // Input with transform readonly count = input(0, { transform: (v) => parseInt(v, 10) }); // Output event readonly changed = output(); // Two-way binding (use sparingly) readonly value = model(''); } ``` ### Usage in Templates ```html ``` ## 5. Standalone Components All new components should be standalone: ```typescript @Component({ selector: 'app-feature', standalone: true, imports: [CommonModule, RouterModule, SharedModule], template: `...`, styles: `...`, }) export class FeatureComponent { // ... } ``` ## 6. Performance Guidelines - Every `@for` must have a stable `track` expression (prefer `item.id` over index) - Extract complex conditions into `computed()` signals - Avoid allocating new arrays/objects in template expressions - Use `OnPush` change detection when appropriate ## 7. Migration Order (Legacy Code) When touching legacy code, migrate in this order: 1. `*ngIf` → `@if` 2. `*ngFor` → `@for` (add `track`) 3. Constructor DI → `inject()` 4. `@Input/@Output` → `input()/output()/model()` 5. Simple state Observables → signals ## 8. PR Checklist - [ ] No mixing of `*ngIf/*ngFor` with `@if/@for` in same logical block - [ ] Every `@for` has a `track` expression - [ ] No unnecessary `subscribe()` calls (use `toSignal`) - [ ] DI uses `inject()` (no new constructor-based DI) - [ ] Inputs/Outputs use `input()/output()/model()` in new code - [ ] No heavy inline computations in templates ``` --- ## 3. `/root/Projects/mobilems-framework/packages/shared/claude-plugins/src/plugins/flow/skills/browser-capture/SKILL.md` ``` --- name: browser-capture description: Manage browser sessions and capture screenshots. Ensures dev server is running, navigates to pages, captures responsive screenshots, and uploads them to temporary storage. allowed-tools: - Bash - Read - Glob - mcp__playwright__browser_navigate - mcp__playwright__browser_snapshot - mcp__playwright__browser_take_screenshot - mcp__playwright__browser_resize - mcp__playwright__browser_close --- # Browser Capture Skill Manage browser sessions for capturing screenshots of web pages. Automatically ensures the development server is running before capturing. ## Capabilities 1. **Server Management**: Check if dev server is running, start if needed 2. **Page Navigation**: Navigate to specified URLs using Playwright 3. **Screenshot Capture**: Take screenshots at multiple viewport sizes 4. **Upload Screenshots**: Upload captured images to maia-api for use in reports ## Server Management ### Read Port Configuration ```bash # Default port location grep -o '"port":[^,}]*' apps/web/project.json | head -1 # Default: 4210 ``` ### Check if Server is Running ```bash lsof -i :4210 -t ``` - If returns PID → server is running - If returns nothing → server needs to be started ### Start Server if Needed ```bash # Start in background npm start & # Wait for server to be ready (check every 2 seconds, max 30 seconds) for i in {1..15}; do curl -s http://localhost:4210 > /dev/null && break sleep 2 done ``` ## Screenshot Capture Process ### 1. Ensure Server is Running Before any screenshot capture: 1. Read port from `apps/web/project.json` (default: 4210) 2. Check if port is occupied: `lsof -i :PORT -t` 3. If not running: `npm start &` and wait ~15 seconds ### 2. Navigate to Page Use `mcp__playwright__browser_navigate` to open the target URL. ``` URL format: http://localhost:{port}/{path} ``` ### 3. Capture at Multiple Viewports Standard viewport sizes: | Name | Width | Height | | ------- | ----- | ------ | | Desktop | 1920 | 1080 | | Tablet | 768 | 1024 | | Mobile | 375 | 667 | Process for each viewport: 1. `mcp__playwright__browser_resize` → set dimensions 2. `mcp__playwright__browser_take_screenshot` → save to temp file ### 4. Upload Screenshots After capturing, delegate upload to `maia-api` agent: ``` Input: /tmp/screenshot-desktop.png Output: { id: "abc123", url: "https://maia-ai-api.smartsoft.biz.pl/files/abc123" } ``` ## Usage Examples ### Capture Single Page ``` Input: { path: "/articles", viewports: ["desktop", "mobile"] } Process: 1. Check server running on port 4210 2. Start if needed, wait for ready 3. Navigate to http://localhost:4210/articles 4. Resize to 1920x1080, screenshot → /tmp/articles-desktop.png 5. Resize to 375x667, screenshot → /tmp/articles-mobile.png 6. Upload both via maia-api agent 7. Return URLs Output: { "desktop": "https://maia-ai-api.smartsoft.biz.pl/files/abc123", "mobile": "https://maia-ai-api.smartsoft.biz.pl/files/def456" } ``` ### Capture Before/After ``` Input: { path: "/profile", phase: "before" | "after", viewports: ["desktop", "mobile"] } Output: { "phase": "before", "desktop": { "id": "abc123", "url": "https://..." }, "mobile": { "id": "def456", "url": "https://..." } } ``` ## Viewport Presets | Preset | Dimensions | Use Case | | ---------- | ---------- | ------------------------ | | `desktop` | 1920x1080 | Full desktop view | | `laptop` | 1366x768 | Common laptop resolution | | `tablet` | 768x1024 | iPad portrait | | `mobile` | 375x667 | iPhone SE/8 | | `mobile-l` | 414x896 | iPhone XR/11 | ## Server Ready Check ```bash # Check if server responds check_server() { local port=$1 local max_attempts=$2 local attempt=1 while [ $attempt -le $max_attempts ]; do if curl -s "http://localhost:$port" > /dev/null 2>&1; then echo "Server ready" return 0 fi echo "Waiting for server... ($attempt/$max_attempts)" sleep 2 ((attempt++)) done echo "Server not ready after $max_attempts attempts" return 1 } check_server 4210 15 ``` ## Output Format When capturing screenshots, return structured data: ```json { "serverStarted": true, "port": 4210, "screenshots": [ { "viewport": "desktop", "dimensions": "1920x1080", "localPath": "/tmp/screenshot-desktop.png", "uploadedId": "abc123", "uploadedUrl": "https://maia-ai-api.smartsoft.biz.pl/files/abc123" }, { "viewport": "mobile", "dimensions": "375x667", "localPath": "/tmp/screenshot-mobile.png", "uploadedId": "def456", "uploadedUrl": "https://maia-ai-api.smartsoft.biz.pl/files/def456" } ] } ``` ## Cleanup After screenshots are used (embedded in Linear comments), cleanup local temp files: ```bash rm /tmp/screenshot-*.png ``` For API cleanup, delegate to `maia-api` agent with collected IDs. ## Error Handling ### Server Won't Start If server fails to start after 30 seconds: 1. Check for port conflicts: `lsof -i :4210` 2. Check npm logs for errors 3. Report error and skip screenshot capture ### Screenshot Fails If Playwright screenshot fails: 1. Verify navigation succeeded 2. Check for JavaScript errors: `mcp__playwright__browser_console_messages` 3. Try alternative viewport 4. Report partial success ### Upload Fails If maia-api upload fails: 1. Verify file exists and has content 2. Retry upload once 3. Report error with local file path as fallback ``` --- ## 4. `/root/Projects/mobilems-framework/packages/shared/claude-plugins/src/plugins/flow/skills/linear-suggestion/SKILL.md` ``` --- name: linear-suggestion description: Create a Linear issue in the "MobileMS Framework" project with a suggestion for agent or skill improvements. allowed-tools: mcp__linear-server__create_issue --- # Linear Suggestion Create a Linear issue in the **MobileMS Framework** project containing a suggestion for improving agent definitions or skills. Used by automated analysis agents to propose changes without blocking the workflow. ## When to Use - When an automated agent (e.g., `shared-parallelization-analyzer`) identifies a recurring pattern that could improve an agent or skill - When proposing orchestrator rule changes based on parallelization analysis - When any non-interactive process needs to suggest framework improvements ## Input Parameters The invoking agent must provide: | Parameter | Required | Description | | ------------- | -------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | `title` | YES | Short summary of the suggestion (max 100 chars) | | `description` | YES | Full markdown description with rationale and proposed changes | | `source` | YES | Agent that generated the suggestion (e.g., `shared-parallelization-analyzer`) | | `targetFile` | NO | Path to the file to be modified (e.g., `agents/shared-impl-orchestrator.md`) | | `category` | NO | One of: `OPTIMIZATION`, `NEW_RULE`, `PATTERN_UPDATE`, `BUG_FIX` | ## Issue Format ### Title ``` [Agent Suggestion] {title} ``` ### Description ```markdown ## Agent Improvement Suggestion **Source agent**: `{source}` **Category**: {category} **Target file**: `{targetFile}` --- {description} --- _This issue was automatically created by `{source}` via the `linear-suggestion` skill._ _Review and apply manually if the suggestion is valid._ ``` ## Process 1. **Format the issue title** with `[Agent Suggestion]` prefix 2. **Build the description** using the template above, inserting all provided parameters 3. **Create the issue** using MCP Linear server: ``` Create issue in project "MobileMS Framework": - Title: [Agent Suggestion] {title} - Description: {formatted description} - Label: agent-evolution ``` 4. **Return the issue ID and URL** to the invoking agent ## Output Format After successful creation, return: ``` Linear issue created: - ID: {issueId} - Title: [Agent Suggestion] {title} - URL: {issueUrl} ``` ## Error Handling If issue creation fails (e.g., MCP Linear server unavailable): - Log the error silently - Do NOT block the parent workflow - Return a failure notice without throwing ``` Linear suggestion skipped: {error reason} ``` ## Example **Input from `shared-parallelization-analyzer`:** ``` title: Add parallel scaffolding rule to orchestrator description: | ### Observation In 3 out of 5 recent tasks, scaffolding agents for independent files (e.g., `angular-component-scaffolder` + `angular-service-builder`) were marked as SEQ despite having no shared output files. ### Proposed Change Add to `shared-impl-orchestrator.md` → Execution Patterns → Parallel vs Sequential: | Scenario | Pattern | Reason | | --------------------------------------- | -------- | ------------------------------- | | Multiple scaffolding for independent files | PARALLEL | No shared output files between scaffolders | ### Evidence - MOB-700: scaffold-component + scaffold-service (independent) - MOB-705: scaffold-directive + scaffold-service (independent) - MOB-710: scaffold-component + scaffold-pipe (independent) source: shared-parallelization-analyzer targetFile: packages/shared/claude-plugins/src/plugins/flow/agents/shared-impl-orchestrator.md category: OPTIMIZATION ``` **Created issue:** ``` Linear issue created: - ID: MOB-815 - Title: [Agent Suggestion] Add parallel scaffolding rule to orchestrator - URL: https://linear.app/mobilems/issue/MOB-815 ``` ``` --- ## 5. `/root/Projects/mobilems-framework/packages/shared/claude-plugins/src/plugins/flow/skills/maia-files-delete/SKILL.md` ``` --- name: maia-files-delete description: Delete a file from Maia API temporary storage. allowed-tools: Bash --- # Maia Files Delete Delete a file from Maia AI API temporary storage to free up space. ## API Endpoint ``` DELETE https://maia-ai-api.smartsoft.biz.pl/files/{id} ``` ## Usage When given a file ID or URL, delete it: ```bash curl -s -X DELETE https://maia-ai-api.smartsoft.biz.pl/files/{id} ``` ## Process 1. Extract ID from input (can be full URL or just ID) 2. Send DELETE request 3. Confirm deletion ## Input Formats Accept either: - Full URL: `https://maia-ai-api.smartsoft.biz.pl/files/abc123` - Just ID: `abc123` ## Output Format After successful deletion: ``` Deleted: {id} ``` ## Batch Deletion When given multiple IDs, delete them all: ```bash for id in id1 id2 id3; do curl -s -X DELETE "https://maia-ai-api.smartsoft.biz.pl/files/$id" echo "Deleted: $id" done ``` ## Error Handling If deletion fails, report the error but continue with remaining files. ``` --- ## 6. `/root/Projects/mobilems-framework/packages/shared/claude-plugins/src/plugins/flow/skills/maia-files-upload/SKILL.md` ``` --- name: maia-files-upload description: Upload a file to Maia API temporary storage. allowed-tools: Bash --- # Maia Files Upload Upload a file to the Maia AI API temporary storage and get a URL for use in Linear comments or markdown. ## API Endpoint ``` POST https://maia-ai-api.smartsoft.biz.pl/files ``` ## Usage When given a file path, upload it: ```bash curl -s -X POST https://maia-ai-api.smartsoft.biz.pl/files \ -F "file=@/path/to/file.png" ``` ## Response ```json { "id": "abc123def456" } ``` ## Result URL ``` https://maia-ai-api.smartsoft.biz.pl/files/{id} ``` ## Process 1. Validate file exists: `test -f "/path/to/file" && echo "OK"` 2. Upload file using curl 3. Extract ID from JSON response 4. Return ID and full URL ## Output Format After successful upload, return: ``` Uploaded: {filename} ID: {id} URL: https://maia-ai-api.smartsoft.biz.pl/files/{id} ``` ## Error Handling If file doesn't exist or upload fails, report the error clearly. ``` --- ## 7. `/root/Projects/mobilems-framework/packages/shared/claude-plugins/src/plugins/flow/skills/review/SKILL.md` ``` --- name: review description: Run parallel multi-dimensional code review using Agent Teams. Spawns specialized reviewers for quality, tests, security, and accessibility/performance. allowed-tools: - Bash - Read - Glob - Grep - Task - TaskCreate - TaskUpdate - TaskList - TaskGet - AskUserQuestion - mcp__linear-server__get_issue - mcp__linear-server__list_comments - mcp__linear-server__create_comment --- # Review Skill Run parallel multi-dimensional code review using Agent Teams. Spawns specialized reviewers for quality, tests, security, and accessibility/performance. ## Execution Checklist Execute each step in order. Do not skip any step marked as MANDATORY. - [ ] **1. Determine review scope** — analyze git diff for changed files + affected projects - [ ] **2. Check team prerequisites** — verify `CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS=1` - [ ] **3. Create Review Board team** — spawn 4 specialized reviewer teammates - [ ] **4. Monitor reviews** — track task completions, handle questions between reviewers - [ ] **5. Synthesize findings** — delegate to `shared-report-synthesizer` - [ ] **6. Post to Linear** — create single review comment in Polish - [ ] **7. Present to user** — show findings with severity ratings - [ ] **8. Cleanup team** ### Task Progress Tracking **MANDATORY**: Use Claude Code's built-in task tracking to provide real-time progress visibility. **Task naming convention**: Every task subject MUST be prefixed with the Linear task ID (or scope): ``` [REVIEW] ``` **At skill start**: Create tasks for each review dimension: ``` TaskCreate({ subject: "[REVIEW] MM-123 Quality review", description: "Lint, type-check, code style, import ordering", activeForm: "[REVIEW] MM-123 Running quality review" }) ``` ## Role **You are an experienced code review lead** with: - Over 10 years of reviewing production code - Deep knowledge of TypeScript, Angular, NestJS security patterns - WCAG 2.1 accessibility expertise - Performance optimization and bundle analysis skills - Ability to coordinate multiple review perspectives You produce reviews that are: - **Thorough**: covering quality, tests, security, and accessibility - **Actionable**: each finding has a clear severity and fix recommendation - **Balanced**: acknowledging good patterns alongside issues - **Prioritized**: critical issues first, minor suggestions last ## Usage ``` /review [linearTaskId] # Review changes for a Linear task /review --staged # Review all staged changes /review --diff=main # Review diff against main branch ``` ## Parameters - `linearTaskId` - Linear task ID (e.g., ENG-123) - `--staged` - Review all staged changes (no Linear task required) - `--diff=` - Review diff against specified branch ## Instructions ### Step 1: Determine Review Scope Analyze the changes to review: **If `linearTaskId` provided:** 1. Fetch task from Linear via MCP 2. Find related branch/commits via `git log --all --grep=""` 3. Determine changed files via `git diff main...` **If `--staged` provided:** 1. Use `git diff --cached --name-only` to find staged files 2. Use `git diff --cached` for full diff **If `--diff=` provided:** 1. Use `git diff ...HEAD --name-only` for changed files 2. Use `git diff ...HEAD` for full diff **Extract:** - List of changed files with change type (added/modified/deleted) - Affected Nx projects (from file paths) - Change statistics (lines added/removed per file) ### Step 2: Check Team Prerequisites **Check `CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS=1`**: If env var is NOT set: → Fall back to sequential review using `shared-verification-orchestrator` agent. ``` Tryb zespołowy niedostępny — brak CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS=1. Uruchamiam sekwencyjną weryfikację... ``` Run the `shared-verification-orchestrator` pipeline and present results. Stop here. **If env var IS set** → Continue to Step 3. ### Step 3: Create Review Board Team Create 4 specialized reviewer tasks and spawn teammates: #### Reviewer 1: Quality Reviewer ``` TaskCreate({ subject: "[REVIEW] Quality review: lint and type-check", description: "Check lint errors, TypeScript type safety, code style, import ordering", activeForm: "[REVIEW] Running quality review" }) ``` **Spawn prompt:** ``` You are the Quality Reviewer in a Review Board for [scope description]. Changed files: [list of changed files with diffs] YOUR FOCUS: - Run linting: `nx lint [project]` for each affected project - Run type-check: `npx tsc --noEmit` for affected projects - Check code style: naming conventions, function length, complexity - Verify import ordering matches ESLint rules - Check for unused imports, dead code, TODO comments - Verify Angular modern APIs (signals, @if/@for, inject()) DELEGATE TO existing agents as needed: - `shared-style-enforcer` for detailed style analysis - `shared-verification-runner` for running lint/type-check commands OUTPUT FORMAT: ## Quality Review Report ### Critical Issues - [issue] — severity: CRITICAL — file: path/to/file.ts:line ### Warnings - [warning] — severity: MEDIUM — file: path/to/file.ts:line ### Passed Checks - [x] Check passed ### Summary Issues found: X critical, Y medium, Z low Recommendation: PASS / PASS_WITH_WARNINGS / BLOCK ``` #### Reviewer 2: Test Reviewer ``` TaskCreate({ subject: "[REVIEW] Test review: coverage and quality", description: "Unit test coverage, test quality, AAA pattern compliance", activeForm: "[REVIEW] Running test review" }) ``` **Spawn prompt:** ``` You are the Test Reviewer in a Review Board for [scope description]. Changed files: [list of changed files with diffs] YOUR FOCUS: - Run unit tests: `nx test [project] --coverage` for affected projects - Check coverage meets 90% minimum for new code - Verify AAA pattern (Arrange-Act-Assert) in all test files - Check test naming: describe('@smartsoft001-mobilems/...'), it('should...') - Verify mock patterns match project conventions - Check for missing edge case tests - Verify no skipped tests (xit, xdescribe) DELEGATE TO existing agents as needed: - `shared-coverage-enforcer` for coverage analysis - `shared-test-runner` for test execution OUTPUT FORMAT: ## Test Review Report ### Critical Issues - [issue] — severity: CRITICAL — file: path/to/file.spec.ts:line ### Warnings - [warning] — severity: MEDIUM — file: path/to/file.spec.ts:line ### Coverage Report | Project | Coverage | Required | Status | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | [project] | 85% | 90% | ❌ BELOW | ### Passed Checks - [x] Check passed ### Summary Issues found: X critical, Y medium, Z low Recommendation: PASS / PASS_WITH_WARNINGS / BLOCK ``` #### Reviewer 3: Security Reviewer ``` TaskCreate({ subject: "[REVIEW] Security review: OWASP and deps", description: "OWASP top 10, dependency audit, secrets detection", activeForm: "[REVIEW] Running security review" }) ``` **Spawn prompt:** ``` You are the Security Reviewer in a Review Board for [scope description]. Changed files: [list of changed files with diffs] YOUR FOCUS: - Check for OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities in changed code: - XSS (innerHTML, bypassSecurityTrust*, template injection) - SQL/NoSQL injection (unsanitized query parameters) - Broken authentication (token handling, session management) - Sensitive data exposure (logging secrets, hardcoded credentials) - CSRF protection - Run dependency audit: `npm audit --audit-level=moderate` - Check for hardcoded secrets, API keys, tokens - Verify input validation at system boundaries - Check for insecure HTTP calls (http:// vs https://) - Review authorization checks in backend code DELEGATE TO existing agents as needed: - `shared-security-scanner` for automated security scanning OUTPUT FORMAT: ## Security Review Report ### Critical Issues - [issue] — severity: CRITICAL — file: path/to/file.ts:line — OWASP: [category] ### Warnings - [warning] — severity: MEDIUM — file: path/to/file.ts:line ### Dependency Audit - Vulnerabilities found: [count by severity] ### Passed Checks - [x] Check passed ### Summary Issues found: X critical, Y medium, Z low Recommendation: PASS / PASS_WITH_WARNINGS / BLOCK ``` #### Reviewer 4: A11y + Performance Reviewer ``` TaskCreate({ subject: "[REVIEW] A11y + Performance review", description: "WCAG 2.1 accessibility, bundle sizes, performance patterns", activeForm: "[REVIEW] Running a11y + performance review" }) ``` **Spawn prompt:** ``` You are the Accessibility & Performance Reviewer in a Review Board for [scope description]. Changed files: [list of changed files with diffs] YOUR FOCUS (Accessibility): - WCAG 2.1 compliance in templates: - Alt text for images - ARIA labels for interactive elements - Keyboard navigation support - Color contrast (check Tailwind classes) - Focus management - Semantic HTML usage - Screen reader compatibility YOUR FOCUS (Performance): - Bundle size impact (new imports, lazy loading) - Change detection optimization (OnPush, signals) - Template performance (trackBy/@for track, computed signals) - Memory leaks (unsubscribed observables, event listeners) - Unnecessary re-renders DELEGATE TO existing agents as needed: - `ui-a11y-validator` for accessibility scanning - `shared-performance-validator` for performance analysis OUTPUT FORMAT: ## A11y + Performance Review Report ### Critical Issues - [issue] — severity: CRITICAL — file: path/to/file.ts:line — type: A11Y/PERF ### Warnings - [warning] — severity: MEDIUM — file: path/to/file.ts:line ### Accessibility Audit - WCAG violations: [count] - Missing ARIA labels: [count] ### Performance Impact - New dependencies added: [list] - Bundle size estimate: [impact] ### Passed Checks - [x] Check passed ### Summary Issues found: X critical, Y medium, Z low Recommendation: PASS / PASS_WITH_WARNINGS / BLOCK ``` ### Step 4: Monitor Reviews While teammates are working: 1. **Track progress** via `TaskList` — check which reviewers have completed 2. **Handle cross-reviewer communication**: - When a reviewer finishes with CRITICAL or HIGH findings, broadcast to other reviewers - Example: Security reviewer finds XSS → notify Quality reviewer to check sanitization 3. **Wait for all reviewers** to complete (or timeout after reasonable period) **Cross-reviewer broadcast logic:** ``` When reviewer finishes: IF findings contain CRITICAL or HIGH severity: FOR each other active reviewer: Send broadcast: "[Reviewer] found [severity] issue: [summary]" Ask them to check related areas ``` ### Step 5: Synthesize Findings After all 4 reviewers complete: 1. **Collect all reports** from reviewer tasks 2. **Delegate to `shared-report-synthesizer`** with: - Team type: REVIEW - All 4 reviewer reports - Linear task ID (if available) The synthesizer will: - Merge findings by category - Deduplicate identical issues - Flag conflicts between reviewers - Produce single Polish-language report ### Step 6: Post to Linear **If `linearTaskId` was provided:** Post the synthesized review report as a comment on the Linear task via MCP. **If `--staged` or `--diff`:** Skip Linear posting (no task to attach to). ### Step 7: Present to User Show the synthesized review results to the user: 1. **Overall verdict**: PASS / PASS_WITH_WARNINGS / BLOCK 2. **Summary table** with per-dimension results 3. **Critical issues** requiring immediate attention 4. **Warnings** for consideration 5. **Passed checks** for confidence ### Step 8: Cleanup Team Mark all review tasks as completed and clean up team resources. ## Fallback: Sequential Review When `CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS=1` is NOT set, run a sequential review: 1. Delegate to `shared-verification-orchestrator` for the full 5-stage pipeline 2. Present results to user 3. Post to Linear if task ID provided This provides the same verification coverage, just without the parallelism and specialization. ## Guidelines 1. **All Linear comments in Polish** — review reports posted to Linear must be in Polish 2. **User-facing output can be in user's language** — terminal output matches user's language 3. **Severity ratings are mandatory** — every finding must have CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW 4. **Recommendations must be actionable** — don't just flag issues, suggest fixes 5. **Respect existing patterns** — don't flag patterns that are established conventions 6. **Consider context** — new code vs modified code may have different standards 7. **Be constructive** — balance criticism with positive observations 8. **Track everything** — use TaskCreate/TaskUpdate for visibility into review progress ## Agent Team Mode (Experimental) **Prerequisite**: `CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS=1` in settings. **Activation**: Automatic when env var is set. **Fallback**: If env var not set, skill runs in standard sequential mode via `shared-verification-orchestrator`. ``` --- ## 8. `/root/Projects/mobilems-framework/packages/shared/claude-plugins/src/plugins/flow/skills/test-e2e/SKILL.md` ``` --- name: test-e2e description: Orchestrate E2E Playwright test creation — scaffold project, inject data-testid attributes, write Page Objects and test specs, and run tests. allowed-tools: - Bash - Read - Write - Edit - Glob - Grep - Task --- # E2E Test Skill Orchestrate the full E2E Playwright testing workflow: ensure project infrastructure exists, inject `data-testid` attributes into changed component templates, write Page Objects and test specs, and run tests. ## Usage ``` /test-e2e [component-paths or 'changed'] ``` ### Parameters - **component-paths**: Explicit paths to component files (e.g., `libs/shared/angular/src/lib/components/contact-form/contact-form.component.ts`) - **`changed`**: Automatically detect changed component templates from `git diff` ## Workflow Execute each step in order: ### Step 1: Identify Target Components **If `changed` is specified:** ```bash # Find changed component templates git diff --name-only HEAD | grep -E "\.component\.(html|ts)$" ``` **If explicit paths are provided:** Use the provided paths directly. **Output:** List of component files to process. ### Step 2: Scaffold E2E Project **Delegate to `e2e-project-scaffolder` agent:** ``` Check if E2E Playwright project exists for the target application. If not, scaffold one with Nx generator, multi-browser config, and base Page Object. ``` The agent will: 1. Check for existing E2E project (idempotent) 2. Scaffold if missing: Playwright config, directory structure, base page 3. Report status **If project already exists:** Continue to Step 3. **If scaffolded:** Continue to Step 3. ### Step 3: Inject `data-testid` Attributes **Delegate to `e2e-data-testid-injector` agent:** ``` Scan the following changed component templates and inject data-testid attributes: Components: - [list of component files from Step 1] Rules: - Naming convention: {component-name}-{element-type}-{identifier} - Target interactive elements only - NEVER overwrite existing data-testid attributes - Preserve template formatting ``` The agent will: 1. Read each component template 2. Identify interactive elements without `data-testid` 3. Inject attributes following naming convention 4. Report injection summary ### Step 4: Write E2E Tests **Delegate to `e2e-test-writer` agent:** ``` Write Playwright E2E tests for the following components: Components: - [list of component files from Step 1] E2E project location: [path from Step 2] Requirements: - Create Page Object per component extending BasePage - Create test spec per component - Use data-testid selectors exclusively - Cover: visibility, interactions, form submissions, navigation, error states - Use Playwright auto-waiting (no manual sleeps) - Cross-reference all selectors against template data-testid values ``` The agent will: 1. Read component `.ts` and `.html` files 2. Create Page Objects in `e2e/src/pages/` 3. Create test specs in `e2e/src/specs/` 4. Cross-reference selectors against templates ### Step 5: Run E2E Tests ```bash # Run E2E tests npx nx e2e {app-name}-e2e ``` **If tests pass:** Report success. **If tests fail:** Analyze failures and fix — delegate back to `e2e-test-writer` if needed. ### Step 6: Report Results Generate a summary report: ```markdown ## E2E Test Report ### Components Processed | Component | Testids Injected | Tests Written | Status | | --------- | ---------------- | ------------- | ------ | | ContactFormComponent | 5 | 6 | PASS | | ArticleListComponent | 3 | 4 | PASS | ### Summary - **E2E Project**: [existed / scaffolded] - **Testids injected**: [total count] - **Tests created**: [total count] - **Test execution**: [PASS / FAIL with details] ### Files Modified | File | Change | | ---- | ------ | | `contact-form.component.html` | Added 5 data-testid attributes | | `e2e/src/pages/contact-form.page.ts` | New Page Object | | `e2e/src/specs/contact-form.spec.ts` | New test spec (6 tests) | ``` ## Rules 1. **Always scaffold first** — ensure E2E project exists before writing tests 2. **Always inject testids first** — ensure selectors exist before writing Page Objects 3. **Use agents for specialized work** — delegate to the right agent for each step 4. **Run tests after writing** — verify tests pass before reporting success 5. **Report comprehensively** — include injection summary, tests created, and execution status ``` --- All 8 files have been read and their complete contents are presented above.

# Review Skill

Run multi-dimensional code review covering quality, tests, security, and performance.

## Usage

```
/review [linearTaskId]
/review --staged
/review --diff=main
```

## Execution Checklist

- [ ] **1. Determine review scope** — analyze git diff for changed files + affected projects
- [ ] **2. Quality review** — lint, type-check, code style, import ordering
- [ ] **3. Test review** — coverage, test quality, AAA pattern compliance
- [ ] **4. Security review** — OWASP top 10, dependency audit, secrets detection
- [ ] **5. Performance review** — bundle impact, change detection, memory leaks
- [ ] **6. Synthesize findings** — merge all findings, deduplicate, prioritize
- [ ] **7. Post to Linear** — create review comment in Polish (if linearTaskId provided)
- [ ] **8. Present to user** — show findings with severity ratings

## Instructions

### Step 1: Determine Review Scope

**If `linearTaskId` provided:**
1. Find related commits via `git log --all --grep="<taskId>"`
2. Determine changed files via `git diff main...<branch>`

**If `--staged`:**
1. Use `git diff --cached --name-only`

**If `--diff=<branch>`:**
1. Use `git diff <branch>...HEAD --name-only`

### Step 2: Quality Review

Delegate to `shared-style-enforcer` agent:
- Run linting: `nx lint <project>` for affected projects
- Run type-check: `npx tsc --noEmit`
- Check import ordering, naming conventions
- Verify Angular modern APIs (signals, @if/@for, inject())

### Step 3: Test Review

Delegate to `shared-test-runner` and `shared-coverage-enforcer`:
- Run tests: `nx test <project> --coverage`
- Check coverage meets 80% minimum
- Verify AAA pattern compliance
- Check test naming: `describe('@smartsoft001/...: ClassName')`

### Step 4: Security Review

Delegate to `shared-security-scanner`:
- Check OWASP Top 10 in changed code
- Run `npm audit`
- Check for hardcoded secrets
- Verify input validation

### Step 5: Performance Review

- Check new imports and bundle impact
- Verify `track` in all `@for` loops
- Check for memory leaks (unsubscribed observables)
- Verify OnPush change detection usage

### Step 6: Synthesize and Report

```markdown
## Code Review Report

### Overall Verdict
[PASS / PASS_WITH_WARNINGS / BLOCK]

### Summary
| Dimension | Critical | Medium | Low | Status |
|-----------|----------|--------|-----|--------|
| Quality | X | Y | Z | PASS/BLOCK |
| Tests | X | Y | Z | PASS/BLOCK |
| Security | X | Y | Z | PASS/BLOCK |
| Performance | X | Y | Z | PASS/BLOCK |

### Critical Issues
- [issue] — file: path/to/file.ts:line

### Warnings
- [warning] — file: path/to/file.ts:line

### Passed Checks
- [x] Check passed

---
_Review generated by Claude Code_
```

## Guidelines

1. **All Linear comments in Polish**
2. **Every finding must have severity rating** — CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW
3. **Recommendations must be actionable** — suggest fixes, not just flag issues
4. **Respect existing patterns** — don't flag established conventions
5. **Be constructive** — balance criticism with positive observations

# E2E Test Skill

Orchestrate the full E2E Playwright testing workflow.

## Usage

```
/test-e2e [component-paths or 'changed']
```

## Workflow

### Step 1: Identify Target Components

**If `changed`:**
```bash
git diff --name-only HEAD | grep -E "\.component\.(html|ts)$"
```

**If explicit paths:** Use provided paths directly.

### Step 2: Scaffold E2E Project

Check if E2E Playwright project exists. If not, scaffold with Nx generator.

### Step 3: Inject `data-testid` Attributes

Scan changed component templates and inject data-testid attributes:
- Naming convention: `{component-name}-{element-type}-{identifier}`
- Target interactive elements only
- Never overwrite existing `data-testid` attributes

### Step 4: Write E2E Tests

For each component:
1. Create Page Object extending BasePage
2. Create test spec
3. Use `data-testid` selectors exclusively
4. Cover: visibility, interactions, form submissions, error states
5. Use Playwright auto-waiting (no manual sleeps)

### Step 5: Run E2E Tests

```bash
npx nx e2e {app-name}-e2e
```

### Step 6: Report Results

```markdown
## E2E Test Report

### Components Processed
| Component | Testids Injected | Tests Written | Status |
|-----------|-----------------|---------------|--------|

### Files Modified
| File | Change |
|------|--------|

### Summary
- E2E Project: [existed / scaffolded]
- Testids injected: [count]
- Tests created: [count]
- Test execution: [PASS / FAIL]
```

## Rules

1. **Always scaffold first** — ensure E2E project exists
2. **Always inject testids first** — ensure selectors exist before writing tests
3. **Run tests after writing** — verify tests pass
4. **Report comprehensively** — include injection summary and execution status

name: linear-suggestion
description: Create a Linear issue with a suggestion for agent or skill improvements.
allowed-tools: mcp__linear-server__create_issue

# Linear Suggestion

Create a Linear issue containing a suggestion for improving agent definitions or skills.

## When to Use

- When an automated agent identifies a recurring pattern that could improve an agent or skill
- When proposing rule changes based on analysis
- When any non-interactive process needs to suggest improvements

## Input Parameters

| Parameter     | Required | Description                                                  |
| ------------- | -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `title`       | YES      | Short summary of the suggestion (max 100 chars)              |
| `description` | YES      | Full markdown description with rationale and proposed changes |
| `source`      | YES      | Agent that generated the suggestion                          |
| `targetFile`  | NO       | Path to the file to be modified                              |
| `category`    | NO       | One of: `OPTIMIZATION`, `NEW_RULE`, `PATTERN_UPDATE`, `BUG_FIX` |

## Issue Format

### Title
```
[Agent Suggestion] {title}
```

### Description
```markdown
## Agent Improvement Suggestion

**Source agent**: `{source}`
**Category**: {category}
**Target file**: `{targetFile}`

---

{description}

---

_This issue was automatically created by `{source}` via the `linear-suggestion` skill._
```

## Process

1. Format the issue title with `[Agent Suggestion]` prefix
2. Build the description using the template
3. Create the issue using MCP Linear server
4. Return the issue ID and URL

## Error Handling

If issue creation fails:
- Log the error silently
- Do NOT block the parent workflow
- Return a failure notice without throwing

2. Build the description using the template
3. Create the issue using MCP Linear server
4. Return the issue ID and URL

## Error Handling

If issue creation fails:
- Log the error silently
- Do NOT block the parent workflow
- Return a failure notice without throwing

# Accessibility Audit Skill

Run accessibility audits on web pages using Playwright and axe-core.

## Audit Process

### 1. Ensure Server is Running

```bash
lsof -i :4200 -t || (npm start &)
```

### 2. Navigate to Page

Use `mcp__playwright__browser_navigate` to open the target URL.

### 3. Capture Accessibility Snapshot

Use `mcp__playwright__browser_snapshot` to get the accessibility tree.

### 4. Run Axe-core Audit

Use `mcp__playwright__browser_evaluate` to inject and run axe-core.

## Report Format

```markdown
## Accessibility Audit Report

**Page**: [URL]
**Standard**: WCAG 2.1 Level AA

### Critical Issues (Must Fix)
| Issue | Elements | WCAG | Fix |
|-------|----------|------|-----|

### Serious Issues (Should Fix)
| Issue | Elements | WCAG | Fix |
|-------|----------|------|-----|

### Passed Checks
- [x] Page has lang attribute
- [x] Headings in correct order
- [x] Form inputs have labels

### Summary
- Violations: X critical, Y serious, Z minor
- Passed: N checks
```

---
name: browser-capture
description: Manage browser sessions and capture screenshots at multiple viewport sizes.
allowed-tools:
  - Bash
  - Read
  - Glob
  - mcp__playwright__browser_navigate
  - mcp__playwright__browser_snapshot
  - mcp__playwright__browser_take_screenshot
  - mcp__playwright__browser_resize
  - mcp__playwright__browser_close
---

# Browser Capture Skill

Capture screenshots of web pages at multiple viewport sizes using Playwright.

## Server Management

```bash
# Check if server is running
lsof -i :4200 -t

# Start if needed
npm start &
for i in {1..15}; do
  curl -s http://localhost:4200 > /dev/null && break
  sleep 2
done
```

## Screenshot Capture Process

### 1. Navigate to Page
Use `mcp__playwright__browser_navigate`.

### 2. Capture at Multiple Viewports

| Name    | Width | Height |
| ------- | ----- | ------ |
| Desktop | 1920  | 1080   |
| Tablet  | 768   | 1024   |
| Mobile  | 375   | 667    |

### 3. Upload Screenshots
Delegate upload to `maia-files-upload` skill.

## Output Format

```json
{
  "screenshots": [
    {
      "viewport": "desktop",
      "dimensions": "1920x1080",
      "localPath": "/tmp/screenshot-desktop.png"
    }
  ]
}
```

---
name: maia-files-upload
description: Upload a file to Maia API temporary storage.
allowed-tools: Bash
---

# Maia Files Upload

Upload a file to the Maia AI API temporary storage and get a URL for use in Linear comments.

## API Endpoint

```
POST https://maia-ai-api.smartsoft.biz.pl/files
```

## Usage

```bash
curl -s -X POST https://maia-ai-api.smartsoft.biz.pl/files \
  -F "file=@/path/to/file.png"
```

## Process

1. Validate file exists: `test -f "/path/to/file"`
2. Upload file using curl
3. Extract ID from JSON response
4. Return ID and full URL

## Output Format

```
Uploaded: {filename}
ID: {id}
URL: https://maia-ai-api.smartsoft.biz.pl/files/{id}
```

name: maia-files-delete
description: Delete a file from Maia API temporary storage.
allowed-tools: Bash
---

# Maia Files Delete

Delete a file from Maia AI API temporary storage.

## API Endpoint

```
DELETE https://maia-ai-api.smartsoft.biz.pl/files/{id}
```

## Usage

```bash
curl -s -X DELETE https://maia-ai-api.smartsoft.biz.pl/files/{id}
```

## Batch Deletion

```bash
for id in id1 id2 id3; do
  curl -s -X DELETE "https://maia-ai-api.smartsoft.biz.pl/files/$id"
  echo "Deleted: $id"
done
```

## Error Handling

If deletion fails, report the error but continue with remaining files.

# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Project Overview

Nx monorepo with shared libraries for Angular, NestJS, and Ionic projects. Organized as publishable @smartsoft001/\* npm packages.

## Technology Stack

- **Angular**: 20.1.0
- **NestJS**: 11.1.5
- **Nx**: 21.3.11
- **TypeScript**: 5.8.3
- **NgRx**: 20.0.1
- **Storybook**: 9.0.17
- **Jest**: 29.7.0
- **pnpm**: 9.11.0

## Project Structure

```
packages/
  auth/           - Authentication (domain, shell/nestjs, shell/dtos, shell/app-services)
  crud/           - CRUD operations (domain, shell/nestjs, shell/angular, shell/dtos, shell/app-services)
  trans/          - Translations (domain, shell/nestjs, shell/dtos, shell/app-services)
  shared/
    angular/      - Shared Angular components, NgRx state management
    nestjs/       - NestJS utilities
    domain-core/  - Base repository patterns
    models/       - Data model decorators
    users/        - User entity definitions
    utils/        - NIP, PESEL, zip-code, password hashing
    mongo/        - MongoDB utilities
    paypal/, payu/, paynow/, revolut/ - Payment integrations
    fb/, google/  - Third-party integrations
    claude-plugins/ - Claude Code plugins (smart@smartsoft)
```

## Agents

**MANDATORY: Always delegate to the appropriate agent.** Do NOT perform tasks manually if an agent exists.

### Shared (`shared-`)

| Agent                      | Use For                             |
| -------------------------- | ----------------------------------- |
| `shared-tdd-developer`     | TDD workflow (RED->GREEN->REFACTOR) |
| `shared-build-verifier`    | Verify build success                |
| `shared-test-runner`       | Run test suites                     |
| `shared-logic-implementer` | Implement business logic            |
| `shared-style-enforcer`    | Enforce ESLint/Prettier             |
| `shared-config-updater`    | Update config files                 |
| `shared-file-creator`      | Create files from templates         |
| `shared-error-handler`     | Standardize error handling          |
| `shared-security-scanner`  | Scan for vulnerabilities            |
| `shared-test-fixer`        | Fix failing tests                   |
| `shared-coverage-enforcer` | Enforce test coverage               |

### Angular (`angular-`)

| Agent                          | Use For                      |
| ------------------------------ | ---------------------------- |
| `angular-component-scaffolder` | Create standalone components |
| `angular-service-builder`      | Create injectable services   |
| `angular-jest-test-writer`     | Write Jest unit tests        |

### NestJS (`nestjs-`)

| Agent                    | Use For                    |
| ------------------------ | -------------------------- |
| `nestjs-service-builder` | Create injectable services |
| `nestjs-module-builder`  | Create NestJS modules      |

## Skills

| Skill            | Use For                             |
| ---------------- | ----------------------------------- |
| `test-unit`      | Jest test conventions (AAA pattern) |
| `nx-conventions` | Nx monorepo patterns and commands   |
| `debug-helper`   | Cross-stack debugging workflows     |

## Common Commands

```bash
# Build
nx build <project-name>
npx nx run-many -t build

# Test
nx test <project-name>
nx run-many --target=test --all
npm test

# Lint & Format
npm run format
nx lint <project-name>

# Storybook
nx storybook angular
nx storybook crud-shell-angular
```

## Key Configuration

- **Package manager**: pnpm
- **Path aliases**: `@smartsoft001/{package}` in `tsconfig.base.json`
- **Component prefix**: `smart` (CRUD shell), `lib` (shared Angular)
- **Project tags**: `scope:crud|auth|trans`, `type:shell|domain`

## Git Conventions

- Conventional commits with commitlint
- Husky pre-commit hooks
- Branch: `main`
- Auto-versioning via GitHub Actions

## Claude Code Plugin: smart@smartsoft

Plugin location: `packages/shared/claude-plugins/src/plugins/smart/`

| Event              | Hooks                                                                   |
| ------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `PreToolUse`       | safety_validator, sensitive file blocker, audit_logger, skill_validator |
| `PostToolUse`      | auto-format, audit_logger                                               |
| `UserPromptSubmit` | audit_logger                                                            |

## Plan implementacji: Aktualizacja Angular 21 + Nx

### Stan obecny

| Pakiet | Obecna wersja | Docelowa wersja |
|--------|--------------|-----------------|
| Angular | 20.1.0 | 21.x |
| Nx | 22.0.3 | najnowszy 22.x lub 23.x |
| TypeScript | 5.8.3 | 5.9.x (jeśli wymagany) |
| NgRx | 20.0.1 | 21.x |
| ng-packagr | 20.1.0 | 21.x |
| zone.js | 0.15.0 | do weryfikacji |
| jest-preset-angular | 14.6.1 | 15.x |
| Storybook | 9.0.17 (+ alpha mix) | **BLOCKER** |
| NestJS | 11.1.5 | bez zmian |

### Blocker: Storybook + Angular 21

Storybook 9.0.17 nie wspiera Angular 21. Pakiety alpha (`@storybook/addon-essentials@9.0.0-alpha.12`, `@storybook/addon-interactions@9.0.0-alpha.10`, `@storybook/core-server@9.0.0-alpha.1`) już powodują niespójności. Przed aktualizacją Angulara trzeba:
1. Albo poczekać na oficjalne wsparcie Storybook dla Angular 21
2. Albo tymczasowo wyłączyć Storybook z pipeline'u i wrócić do niego później

### Fazy implementacji

#### Faza 1: Przygotowanie (bez breaking changes)
1. **Ujednolicenie wersji Storybook** — zamienić wszystkie alpha na stabilne 9.0.17 lub usunąć niespójne wersje
2. **Ujednolicenie @typescript-eslint** — `7.18.0` vs `8.36.0` w devDependencies, wybrać jedną wersję
3. **Uruchomić pełny test suite** — `nx run-many -t test --all` aby mieć baseline
4. **Uruchomić pełny build** — `nx run-many -t build` aby potwierdzić stabilność

#### Faza 2: Migracja Nx
5. **`nx migrate latest`** — wygenerować migracje Nx
6. **Przejrzeć `migrations.json`** — zidentyfikować breaking changes
7. **`nx migrate --run-migrations`** — uruchomić migracje
8. **Weryfikacja** — build + testy po migracji Nx

#### Faza 3: Migracja Angular 21
9. **`ng update @angular/core@21 @angular/cli@21`** — aktualizacja Angular core
10. **Aktualizacja `@angular-devkit/*`** — build-angular, core, schematics → 21.x
11. **Aktualizacja `@schematics/angular`** → 21.x
12. **Aktualizacja `ng-packagr`** → 21.x
13. **Aktualizacja `@angular/cdk`** → 21.x
14. **Weryfikacja TypeScript** — sprawdzić czy Angular 21 wymaga nowszego TS

#### Faza 4: Aktualizacja zależności Angular
15. **NgRx** → 21.x (effects, signals, store)
16. **zone.js** — wersja kompatybilna z Angular 21
17. **jest-preset-angular** → wersja kompatybilna z Angular 21
18. **ngx-*** pakiety — `ngx-color-picker`, `ngx-cookie`, `ngx-editor`, `ng-dynamic-component`, `ng-lazyload-image` — sprawdzić kompatybilność
19. **@ngx-translate/core** — sprawdzić wsparcie dla Angular 21

#### Faza 5: Weryfikacja
20. **Build wszystkich bibliotek** — `nx run-many -t build`
21. **Testy jednostkowe** — `nx run-many -t test --all`
22. **Lint** — `nx run-many -t lint`
23. **Ręczne testy** — sprawdzić kluczowe komponenty CRUD

#### Faza 6: Storybook (warunkowa)
24. **Sprawdzić dostępność** `@storybook/angular` kompatybilnego z Angular 21
25. **Jeśli dostępny** — zaktualizować wszystkie pakiety Storybook
26. **Jeśli niedostępny** — tymczasowo wyłączyć Storybook targets w `project.json` i utworzyć follow-up task

### Ryzyka

| Ryzyko | Prawdopodobieństwo | Mitygacja |
|--------|-------------------|-----------|
| Storybook niekompatybilny z Angular 21 | **Wysokie** | Tymczasowe wyłączenie, follow-up task |
| Pakiety ngx-* niekompatybilne | Średnie | Szukać forków lub alternatyw |
| Breaking changes w NgRx 21 | Niskie | NgRx zazwyczaj ma łagodne migracje |
| TypeScript version bump | Średnie | Zaktualizować TS jako pierwszy krok Fazy 3 |

### Rekomendacja

**Rozpocząć od Fazy 1-2 (Nx)**, ponieważ `nx migrate` automatycznie zaktualizuje wiele zależności Angular. Następnie dokończyć ręcznie Fazę 3-5. Storybook (Faza 6) traktować jako osobny krok warunkowy.

---
_Plan wygenerowany automatycznie przez Claude Code na podstawie analizy repozytorium._

22. **Aktualizacja addonów** — `@storybook/addon-essentials`, `@storybook/addon-interactions` → 10.x
23. **Usunięcie `@storybook/core-server`** (alpha) — sprawdzić czy nadal potrzebny w v10
24. **Aktualizacja `@storybook/test-runner`** → wersja kompatybilna z v10
25. **Aktualizacja `@nx/storybook`** — upewnić się że wspiera Storybook 10
26. **Weryfikacja konfiguracji** — `.storybook/main.ts` w `crud/shell/angular` i `shared/angular`
27. **Usunięcie `@storybook/jest`** i `@storybook/testing-library` — w Storybook 10 mogą być zastąpione przez `@storybook/test`

#### Faza 6: Weryfikacja końcowa
28. **Build wszystkich bibliotek** — `nx run-many -t build`
29. **Testy jednostkowe** — `nx run-many -t test --all`
30. **Lint** — `nx run-many -t lint`
31. **Storybook build** — `nx build-storybook angular` + `nx build-storybook crud-shell-angular`
32. **Ręczne testy** — sprawdzić kluczowe komponenty CRUD

### Ryzyka

| Ryzyko | Prawdopodobieństwo | Mitygacja |
|--------|-------------------|-----------|
| Storybook 10 breaking changes w API | Średnie | Przejrzeć migration guide Storybook 9→10 |
| Pakiety ngx-* niekompatybilne z Angular 21 | Średnie | Szukać forków lub alternatyw |
| Breaking changes w NgRx 21 | Niskie | NgRx zazwyczaj ma łagodne migracje |
| TypeScript version bump | Średnie | Zaktualizować TS jako pierwszy krok Fazy 3 |
| @nx/storybook nie wspiera v10 | Niskie | Sprawdzić release notes Nx |

### Rekomendacja

**Rozpocząć od Fazy 1-2 (Nx)**, ponieważ `nx migrate` automatycznie zaktualizuje wiele zależności Angular. Następnie Faza 3-4 (Angular 21 + zależności), potem Faza 5 (Storybook 10). Aktualizacja Storybook do v10 rozwiązuje problem kompatybilności z Angular 21.

---

file.content:
---
name: impl
description: Implement plans from Linear task comments. Iteratively processes subtasks with TDD, agent orchestration, and 3x3 rule checkpoints.
allowed-tools:
  - Bash
  - Read
  - Edit
  - Write
  - Glob
  - Grep
  - AskUserQuestion
  - TaskCreate
  - TaskUpdate
  - TaskList
  - TaskGet
  - mcp__linear-server__get_issue
  - mcp__linear-server__list_issues
  - mcp__linear-server__list_comments
  - mcp__linear-server__create_comment
  - mcp__linear-server__update_issue
  - mcp__linear-server__list_issue_statuses
---

# Implementation Skill

Implement plans from Linear task comments. For tasks with subtasks, iteratively implement each subtask that is in "To Do" status.

## Usage

```
/impl [linearTaskId]
```

## Parameters

- `linearTaskId` - Linear task ID (e.g., ENG-123)

## Execution Checklist

Execute each step in order. Do not skip any step marked as MANDATORY.

### Initial Setup

- [ ] **1. Fetch task and subtasks** — get task details, subtasks, and their statuses

### Per Subtask (repeat for each "To Do" subtask)

- [ ] **2. Set status to "In Progress"** — update subtask status via MCP (subtasks only, not parent)
- [ ] **3. Fetch implementation plan** — find `## Implementation Plan` in task comments
- [ ] **4. MANDATORY: Agent orchestration plan** — determine which agents to use, post plan to Linear
- [ ] **5. Check cross-package blockers** — if blockers found, set status to Blocked and skip
- [ ] **6. MANDATORY: Implement with 3x3 rule** — pause every 3 changes, use `shared-tdd-developer` for ALL code (RED -> GREEN -> REFACTOR)
- [ ] **7. Verify implementation** — run tests, lint, build check
- [ ] **8. Create completion comment** — post implementation report to Linear
- [ ] **9. MANDATORY: Write reports in Polish** — all Linear comments must be in Polish language
- [ ] **10. Update status** — set to "In Review" if completed
- [ ] **11. Pause for user confirmation** — ALWAYS wait before proceeding to next subtask

### Finalization

- [ ] **12. Final summary** — present summary of all subtasks with statuses
- [ ] **13. ALWAYS pause** — wait for user confirmation at end

### Task Progress Tracking

**MANDATORY**: Use Claude Code's built-in task tracking.

**Task naming convention**: `<linearTaskId> <step description>`

## Role

**You are a highly experienced senior TypeScript developer** with:

- TypeScript expert - advanced types, generics, decorators
- NestJS expert - modules, DI, guards, interceptors
- Angular expert - signals, standalone components, NgRx
- Writing clean, readable, and well-tested code

## Instructions

### Step 1: Fetch Linear Task and Subtasks

Use MCP Linear server to fetch task details, check for subtasks, filter "To Do" status.

### Step 2: Process Tasks Iteratively

For each task to implement (subtasks in "To Do" status, or parent task if no subtasks):

#### Step 2a: Set Status to "In Progress"

For subtasks only, update status to "In Progress".

#### Step 2b: Fetch Implementation Plan

Look for comment containing `## Implementation Plan` or `## Implementation Plan for Subtask`.

Extract: Implementation Steps, Files to Modify, New Files, Testing Strategy.

**If no plan found**: Create a comment noting that no plan was found and skip this task.

#### Step 2c: Agent Orchestration Plan

Before starting implementation, create an agent orchestration plan:

1. Identify required agents based on task type
2. Define execution order (parallel vs sequential)
3. Plan TDD cycles for each implementation unit

Post the orchestration plan as a comment on the Linear task.

**IMPORTANT**: `shared-tdd-developer` is MANDATORY for ALL code implementation.

#### Step 2d: Check for Cross-Package Blockers

If the plan references changes in other packages that haven't been implemented yet, set status to "Blocked" and skip.

#### Step 2e: Implement the Plan (with 3x3 Rule)

Follow the implementation steps, applying the **3x3 Rule**:

**3x3 Rule**: After every 3 changes, STOP and:

1. Summarize what was completed in the last 3 changes
2. Present the next 3 planned steps
3. Wait for user confirmation before continuing

**3x3 Checkpoint Format:**

```markdown
## Checkpoint (3x3)

### Completed (last 3 changes)

1. [Change 1] - `path/to/file1.ts`
2. [Change 2] - `path/to/file2.ts`
3. [Change 3] - `path/to/file3.ts`

### Next 3 Steps

1. [ ] [Next step 1]
2. [ ] [Next step 2]
3. [ ] [Next step 3]

### Progress

- Steps completed: X / Y
- Remaining: Z steps

Continue? (yes/no/adjust)
```

#### Step 2f: Verify Implementation

1. **Run relevant tests**: `nx test <project-name>`
2. **Run linting**: `nx lint <project-name>`
3. **Build check**: `nx build <project-name>`

#### Step 2g: Create Completion Comment

Post an Implementation Report to Linear:

```markdown
## Implementation Report

### Status

[Completed / Partially Completed / Blocked]

### Completed Items

- [item 1]
- [item 2]

### Files Modified

| File | Changes |
| ---- | ------- |

### Files Created

| File | Purpose |
| ---- | ------- |

### Test Results

- Unit tests: [pass/fail]
- Lint: [pass/fail]
- Build: [pass/fail]

---

_Report generated by Claude Code_
```

#### Step 2h: Update Status

Set to "In Review" if completed, "To Do" if partial, "Blocked" if external blockers.

#### Step 2i: Pause After Subtask

**ALWAYS wait for user confirmation before proceeding to next subtask.**

### Step 3: Final Summary

Present summary of all subtasks and their statuses.

## Verification Pipeline

When verification fails, delegate to appropriate agent:

| Failure Type  | Delegate To              |
| ------------- | ------------------------ |
| Lint errors   | shared-style-enforcer    |
| Type errors   | shared-logic-implementer |
| Test failures | shared-test-fixer        |
| Build errors  | shared-build-verifier    |

## Pause Points

| Pause Point          | When                  | What to Show           |
| -------------------- | --------------------- | ---------------------- |
| **3x3 Checkpoint**   | After every 3 changes | Summary + next steps   |
| **Subtask Complete** | After each subtask    | Summary + next subtask |
| **Task Complete**    | After all subtasks    | Final summary          |
| **Blocker Found**    | When dependency found | Blocker details        |

**Never proceed automatically** - always wait for explicit user confirmation.

## Guidelines

1. **Use `shared-tdd-developer` for ALL code** - TDD is mandatory (RED -> GREEN -> REFACTOR)
2. **Write comments in Polish** - all Implementation Reports in Polish
3. **Follow the 3x3 Rule** - pause every 3 changes
4. **Pause after each subtask** - wait for user confirmation
5. **Don't modify parent task status** - only subtask statuses
6. **Run tests before marking complete** - ensure tests pass
7. **Follow existing patterns** - match codebase conventions
8. **Ask for clarification** - if plan is unclear, ask before proceeding

---

**Important**: Always confirm with the user before starting implementation if there are any questions about the plan.

## Plan orkiestracji agentów

### Podejście
Zadanie dotyczy aktualizacji zależności (infrastruktura/konfiguracja), nie wymaga TDD. Implementacja sekwencyjna — faza po fazie.

### Agenci do użycia

| Faza | Agent | Cel |
|------|-------|-----|
| 1-5 | **shared-config-updater** | Aktualizacja package.json, nx.json, tsconfig |
| 2 | **shared-build-verifier** | Weryfikacja build po migracji Nx |
| 3-4 | **shared-build-verifier** | Weryfikacja build po Angular 21 |
| 5 | **shared-config-updater** | Aktualizacja konfiguracji Storybook |
| 6 | **shared-build-verifier** + **shared-test-runner** | Weryfikacja końcowa |

### Kolejność
Sekwencyjnie: Faza 1 → 2 → 3 → 4 → 5 → 6, z checkpoint 3x3 po każdych 3 zmianach.

---

| 6 | **shared-build-verifier** + **shared-test-runner** | Weryfikacja końcowa |

### Kolejność
Sekwencyjnie: Faza 1 → 2 → 3 → 4 → 5 → 6, z checkpoint 3x3 po każdych 3 zmianach.
